channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Sun, 05 Jul 2026 12:58:03 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Chants, tears & prayers: Massive crowd gathers in Tehran for Ali Khamenei funeral - watch
item.description: Millions of Iranians are participating in extensive funeral ceremonies for Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in Tehran, following his death in an airstrike. The Grand Mosalla complex is overwhelmed with mourners paying respects, with prayers held for Khamenei and family members killed alongside him. Chants of "Death to America!" and "Death to Israel!" echoed, reflecting regional tensions. The ongoing events are being closely monitored amid a sensitive geopolitical climate.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Charlie Kirk murder case: Utah prosecutors to argue Tyler Robinson should stand trial
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'We are heroic people with heroic spirit': Donald Trump marks America's 250th Independence Day
item.description: US President Donald Trump marked America's 250th independence anniversary with a patriotic speech on the National Mall, celebrating the nation's enduring spirit and strength. Despite severe storms briefly disrupting the event and forcing an evacuation, thousands gathered for the occasion. Trump lauded veterans and the country's founding principles, while also weaving in partisan themes ahead of upcoming elections. Celebrations across the US were impacted by extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Why did Kai Cenat disappear from streaming? Everything ahead of his July 6 return stream
item.description: Kai Cenat will return to streaming on July 6 at 8 PM EST after spending months away from live content. During the break, he focused on his fashion brand Vivet, personal growth and creative projects. His comeback stream is expected to reveal key Streamer University 2026 details and will likely be his first simultaneous broadcast on Twitch and YouTube.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Candace Owens accuses Andrew Kolvet of ‘lying’ over Charlie Kirk claim in X dispute
item.description: Candace Owens and Andrew Kolvet are publicly disputing what was said during a past private conversation involving Charlie Kirk. Owens says Kolvet told her about an offer to help take Turning Point USA “to the next level,” while Kolvet says that never happened and has demanded proof. No independent evidence has been publicly released by either side so far.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Is Nocturnal Kent dead? Looksmaxxing streamer sparks concern after alleged brain aneurysm rumors
item.description: Rumors about looksmaxxing streamer Nocturnal Kent's alleged death spread rapidly across social media after an image claiming to show him in an ICU bed surfaced online. Posts also alleged he suffered a brain aneurysm. However, no official statement from his family, representatives, or authorities has confirmed the reports. Until verified information is released, the claims remain unconfirmed, and fans have been urged to avoid sharing speculation as fact.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Watch: New York's Brooklyn Bridge catches fire during July 4 fireworks display
item.description: During Macy's Fourth of July fireworks extravaganza, the stunning Brooklyn Bridge briefly caught fire, sending flames and dark smoke billowing up as fireworks lit up the night sky above. Eyewitnesses reported that the fire began modestly but intensified quickly, with additional explosions reportedly triggered by the blaze. The dramatic scene unfolded over approximately one minute, leaving uncertainty about any potential damage to the historic structure.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump offers to help end Ukraine war during 90-minute call with Putin, Zelenskyy
item.description: US President Trump has offered to help resolve the Ukraine conflict following separate calls with Russian President Putin and Ukrainian President Zelenskyy. While Russia claims battlefield advances, Ukraine disputes these assertions. Trump's envoys are set to continue mediation efforts ahead of the upcoming NATO summit, where the stalled peace talks and the ongoing war are expected to be key discussion points.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump delays $5m sexual abuse payout to Jean Carroll; latter senses conspiracy
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Xi says ready to work with Kim for 'long-term, sound and stable' China-North Korea ties
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping has expressed readiness to work with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to foster long-term, stable bilateral ties. This comes amid escalating regional tensions and Pyongyang's deepening military collaboration with Russia. Xi's message, in response to Kim's congratulatory note, highlighted the historical bond and commitment to strengthening their strategic partnership, underscoring China's crucial economic role for North Korea.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Lets have some fun': Trump says he'll deliver July 4 speech 'no matter what' despite storms
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: President Trump extols America, rails at communism in USA 250 celebrations
item.description: Speaking at Mount Rushmore, President Trump warned against a resurgent "communist menace," including from "newcomers" embracing opposing ideologies. He urged Americans to protect founding freedoms, linking progressive Democratic victories to this threat. Trump vowed to "vanquish communism quickly" and send those embracing it "away," asserting America will never be communist, as he rallied supporters ahead of midterm elections.

item.title: Mass grief at funeral projects hardline grip on post-war Iran
item.description: Tens of thousands mourned Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and his family in Tehran following his death in early airstrikes of the US-Israeli war. The nation is staging week-long funeral processions, showcasing public devotion to the Islamic Republic. Mourners expressed deep anger and vowed revenge against the United States, highlighting the ongoing tensions and the symbolic significance of martyrdom in Iran's theocratic system.

item.title: Russia says it has taken five villages in Ukraine
item.description: Russia's defence ministry claims to have captured five villages in Ukraine's eastern regions, including four in Kharkiv and one in Donetsk. However, Ukraine's President Zelensky vehemently denies Russia's assertion of taking the strategically vital city of Kostiantynivka, calling it another fabrication. This city is crucial for Ukraine's defence line in the industrial Donetsk region, and its capture would significantly aid Russia's northern advance.

item.title: Indian student drowns in Germany; four questioned
McDonald's, bus station turn quake clinics in Venezuela
item.description: Following devastating earthquakes in Venezuela, a McDonald's in La Guaira has transformed into a vital clinic, treating hundreds daily for critical conditions like hypertensive crises and anxiety. With hospitals overwhelmed and infrastructure destroyed, makeshift centers in places like bus terminals are the only hope for thousands. Doctors, like those who survived the quakes themselves, are working tirelessly amidst unimaginable devastation to provide essential care.

item.title: 'JUST&T MARRIED' Swift, Kelce host massive New York wedding bash
item.description: Pop sensation Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce have tied the knot in a star-studded ceremony at Madison Square Garden. The surprise wedding, officiated by Adam Sandler, saw Swift's brother as man of honor and Jason Kelce as best man. The couple, who donated $26 million to charity, celebrated their union amidst a throng of fans and celebrities, marking a new chapter in their widely publicized romance.

item.title: Cops in Canada stopped a car for dangerous driving, found a gun, drugs and Indian-origin criminal inside
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Bulgarian proverb of the day: "If you don't patch your old cloth, you will not wear a new one", an age-old wisdom on management
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Russian proverb of the day 'Don't look a gift horse in the mouth' teaches us how to accept a gift with gratitude, humility instead of finding fault in it
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
